headline: Malaysia First Dedicated Cycling Highway Opens in Penang
context: Penang opened Malaysia first dedicated cycling highway in February 2026 a 12km separated path connecting George Town to Batu Ferringhi.

cards.big: Malaysia first cycling highway opens 12km route in Penang February 2026
cards.sub: Separated path with dedicated signals connects George Town to Batu Ferringhi

cards.big: Construction cost RM42 million including 3 elevated crossings over main roads
cards.sub: Daily usage reached 4,800 cyclists in first month exceeding 3,000 projection

cards.big: Cycling commuter mode share in corridor increased from 1.2% to 3.8%
cards.sub: Adjacent property values rose 8% within 3 months of highway opening

cards.big: Plan to extend network to 200km across Penang island by 2030
cards.sub: Federal government contributed zero to the project which was fully state-funded

cards.big: Cycling infrastructure shows whether state-level initiative can pioneer national transport change
Malaysia's first dedicated cycling highway in Penang. One corridor does not make a network.
